segment-text: Olivia Milch & Gary Ross
EXT. NICHOLS WOMEN'S PRISON - DAY

Early spring, sparse moments of green against the sterile grey of the correctional facility. Straining against the bleakness, small flowers start to bloom. The gate clanks open. DEBBIE OCEAN, 40's, impossibly chic for having just done a stint in the slammer -- suit pressed, hair shiny, struts out. Her gait suggests an unpleasant if not untenable sojourn. She greets the world as if it has been waiting for her, allowing herself a moment of digestion -- her posture relaxes, she exhales, she closes her eyes. She hears before she sees the waiting engine of a classic Mercedes, '55, baby Blue. LINUS (Matt Damon) drapes a single hand on the steering wheel but it's a forced nonchalance that could never come close to its owner. Danny Ocean Lite. Deb...
Debbie keeps walking. He cruises up next to her.
Where're you going?
(without looking back) I have $35 Linus, I can go wherever I want.
I'll give you a ride.
(slows/turns) He left you his car? No.
Well -- he knew I loved it...
That's not the same thing.
(seriously/intoned) He would have wanted me to drive it.
She rolls her eyes. Hot day. Long way to walk. So does Linus. He REVS the car in neutral. Deb slows.
Don't do that, ok.
She crosses to the passenger's side and climbs in. INT. CAR - DRIVING - TEN MINUTES LATER
When's the funeral?
Tomorrow at noon. Where?
Really nice place... (off her look) Queens.
Linus changes lanes. Starts moving over toward the right...
Where are you going? I-95. Why?
DEBBIE
We thought -- it might be a good idea to see your folks.
Who thought?
You know...
Eleven of you?
Rusty's in England.
Debbie reaches out and JERKS THE WHEEL crossing two lanes of traffic. They head toward New York.
That is -- dangerous!
We're going to Manhattan.
They're not gonna like that.
Anything else "they" want?
As a matter of fact...
He doesn't finish. Knows he spilled too much.
Saul wants to tell you.
Goddammit, Linus.
He left you a note. Danny did?
Let me see it.
Well that's just it. In the confusion with him "passing," and clearing out the "effects" cause we're the ones who have to deal with the "effects..."
Where's the note?
We're not sure.
You lost it?
It'll turn up.
You lost my dead brother's note to me.
Deb turns.
I know what it said.
It said he doesn't want you to do it. Do what? The job.
LINUS
You know about that?
Well not exactly. But the way he was talking, it sounded pretty clear...
Jesus Christ.
He was worried about you Deb. He doesn't want you to go back to -you know...

They have come to a stop on the George Washington Bridge and all at once, Deb FLINGS OPEN THE DOOR. She steps out into bumper to bumper traffic heading toward Manhattan, leaving Linus stuck in the car.
Wait. Please!
EXT. NEW YORK CITY - AFTERNOON
Teeming city life. Blur and shocks of colors. come to life in a modern context.
INT. BERGDORF'S - DAY

Debbie peruses, approaches the cosmetics counter. The salesperson is consumed with a COSTUMER. Debbie subtly grabs a few bottles of face cream and continues looking, developing an air of impatience. Once the salesperson finishes -DEBBIE Excuse me -- I'd like to return these.
Do you have your
(demonstrating) No but they're unopened, haven't been touched -SALESPERSON I can't accept a return without a
They're sealed, brand new -SALESPERSON I understand but we have a strict return policy and cannot accept -DEBBIE This is ridiculous. a week ago.
Ma'am -- I need a receipt.
Fine. I'll keep them. Jesus. Can I at least get a bag to carry it all?
As the salesperson hands her a bag with a curt smile -CUT TO:
EXT. PLAZA HOTEL - LATER
Debbie approaches with a Bergdorf's bag full of products -Bubble bath, face cream, lotion -- wearing an obviously stolen pair of sunglasses.
INT. PLAZA HOTEL - LOBBY
A buzz around the reception desk. A clerk is checking out a couple in their early forties. They have rolling luggage and computer cases.
We're checking out of room 2814.
DIFFERENT ANGLE
Around the corner at reception desk, Debbie lurks pretending
Certainly, Mr. Crandall. you had a pleasant stay. Terrific.
We hope
HOTEL GUEST
My name is Monica. If there's anything else I can do or arrange transportation...
I think we're all set.
(handing him the receipt) Thank you for staying with us.
Debbie glances up as... THE COUPLE Wheels their luggage toward the door.
EXT. SIDEWALK
The Crandall's climb into an Uber headed toward JFK.
INT. LOBBY
Debbie lounges back in a large overstuffed chair.

(into the phone) Hi, this is Mrs. Crandall, we just checked out of 2814. Can I speak to Monica, please? (beat) Oh, hi Monica -- thanks so much. Something has come up and we're going to need the room another night. (a beat) Oh, bless you. We're just going to grab quick bite. Could you get the maid in there now? (beat)
INT. HALLWAY - PLAZA - CONTINUOUS
The elevator door opens. Debbie steps out, spots the maid's cart. Bingo. She heads off toward 2814.
INT. HOTEL ROOM - PLAZA - CONTINUOUS
The MAID fixes pillows. She looks up as Debbie enters.
Hi. Sorry. Can you just finish this later? I've just got to get off my feet. Of course.
Sorry.
She gathers the rest of her things and hurries toward the door. Wait.
She crosses to the maids cart and grabs a handful of chocolates.
INT. BATHROOM - HOTEL ROOM - PLAZA - LATER
Debbie luxuriates in a bath with all of New York out a huge picture window beside her. The lights glisten. The planes glide by. She sinks a little lower in the warm water. Closes her eyes...
INT. TRENDY CLUB. NIGHT

CATE late 30's, dressed in all black at a landing, hovering above her club. It's packed with kids and sweat. She glances over at the bar. A FEMALE BARTENDER Is pouring Grey Goose into a tumbler. She catches Cate's eye who shakes her head. The woman, chastised, replaces the Grey Goose and grabs a bottle of well vodka... MOVING WITH CATE She scans the floor: hormones and sweat. In the far corner Lebron James is towering above the throng, dancing. Cate descends the stairs and crosses to one of her "Bottle Girls."
Did you get him yet?
No. I will.

Cate looks at her with judgement: "C'mon." her cell phone hurries across the floor. The girl grabs CATE wanders toward the back of the club and spots a girl smoking in the doorway to the alley. She crosses to her and the girl immediately drops her cigarette, grinding it out on the ground.
On a break?
Yeah.
How's the line?
It's pretty good.
Is it down the block?
(busted) Not quite.
You know I need a long line right? Sorry.
I just got cold.
Here.
Take this.
She removes her scarf, wraps it around the girl's neck then smiles. It's a "get-the fuck-out-there smile," but still a smile.

ACROSS THE CLUB... Lebron is seriously into the music when the Bottle Girl comes up and tries to take a selfie. (The height differential is problematic.) He squats lower and extends the camera... CATE... Watching it.
(to her bouncer) Make sure she pushes that out now. Hashtag?
How bout our address.
AND SHE IS MOVING AGAIN, this time toward the VIP area where a YOUNG MAN is holding court with three women and a bottle of Dom. Heavily tatted and well toned. Cate glares -- he looks up...
(beat) They said I could. Who?
The guy at the door.
Instead of buying-off my doorman why don't you pay your bill from last time?
(scared)
She turns to leave and gets a text. she walks. Cate glances down while CELL PHONE TEXT MESSAGE: FROM JLBRD: "Where is the fkng cemetery? O'clock?" SHE smiles walking away...
INT. PLAZA HOTEL SUITE - MORNING
Debbie wakes up to see Manhattan doing the same out her window. Dawn breaks over the skyline as she rises, stretches, and takes in the view -- her first good night's sleep in four years.
BERGDORF'S
She enters the same revolving doors as she did yesterday, disappearing for a beat... Now dressed in a tasteful black suit with sensible shoes.
A CEMETERY
Gravestones compressed in a long lens and stretching to the horizon. A massive 747 lands in the background. DIFFERENT ANGLE.
CEMETERY Black SUV's roll through the gates. INT. FUNERAL CHAPEL
Everyone is there. BASHER, Linus, SAUL, The MALLOY BROTHERS, even YEN. THE COFFIN IS WHITE TOO With gold handles. A lovely touch of Vegas. IN THE PULPIT REUBEN (Elliott Gould) is eulogizing:
Danny Ocean lived a rich full life. By any measure, he was a success.
There is more rustling and a few eye rolls.
Whether in business, not all of which we can discuss today... A few sighs of relief.
Or as a loving son, to Dennis and Darlene.
Everyone turns to acknowledge DENNIS OCEAN, seated on the
Stares straight ahead.
Doesn't glance at her father.
Or as a brother.
She looks up. Shakes her head: don't you dare.
(stumbles) Who -- had a sister. As -brothers do...
INT. CHAPEL - LATER...
People are milling around outside the chapel. approaches Saul (Carl Reiner.)
Hello, Saul.
Sweetie. Oh my gosh. (kisses) You look great.
No I don't.
Well -- you made it. Yeah.
Listen to me for a minute... Don't.
It killed him, Debbie. it killed him.
He had a genetic valve disorder.
Well it didn't help. good for you either. do this. Do what? And it's no Please don't
Whatever it is he wouldn't tell us you were gonna do.
Debbie smiles -- doesn't respond. She leans over and kisses Saul on the cheek.
EXT. CEMETERY
Hot day. Birds. She takes off her jacket. Funny how a graveyard can feel like the country. Basher (Don Cheadle) comes up beside her.
'ello love. You too? No...
Not me.
She smiles at him.
Hear it's good though.
He told you?
Just said it was "somethin'." that it was pretty amazing.
He said that?
Mmm. He said it was insane and you'd end up back in jail but it was pretty brilliant. Sinks in.
You coming back to the house? Nah.
She motions toward the gang. He nods.
Sure. (beat) One question.
What's that?
(leans in/a wink) Why'd you tell him, love?
CLOSE UP.

The one question she can't answer. It lingers with her. gives her a kiss on the cheek and heads on his way. He CAR HORN Two short beeps. Deb turns... HER POV The smoked window of a black SUV lowers revealing -CATE Blonde, stylish, hair perfectly messy. the graveyard. Her car radio fills
INT. CAR
Deb climbs in the passengers side as Cate turns down the music. Cate grabs her friend's face. Big kiss on the cheek. Easy.
I've been in the slammer. Really?
Hadn't heard.
Thanks. That was stressful... I'm sure.
Everybody trying to get me to go straight.
Kind of cheeky. too.
They're crooks
No kidding.
Cate swings the car onto the expressway. Deb takes off her shoes. Puts her bare feet on the dashboard and rubs them... Deb puts her bare feet on the dashboard.
You get the credit line? Not yet.
Don't know what it's for. (off her look) Please don't do that? Do what?
Cate imitates her expression.
Oh. That's my "just spent fouryears in jail and my partner lets me down face."

I'm not your partner yet. (cell phone rings) Hello? (beat) I don't care Jeffrey... No I don't want to talk to her... (another beat) 'Cause it's not her job anymore. It was her job until she got fired, now it's not her job... (listens) She can write me five letters, she's not getting her job back. (beat) Because she stole from me Jeffrey and people who steal are not trustworthy.
On that, she happens to lock eyes Deb who starts to laugh. Cate can't help it smiles too. Hangs up.
I mean "normal" people who steal. Not...
Like professionals? Yeah.
Them.
INT. ABANDONED THEATER - DAY
The lights switch on, illuminating a huge empty space. It's gorgeous in its decay. Carved wood and peeling plaster. The gold leaf is sporadic.
This is great.
Try heating it.
She flips on some more lights.
There's a room for you upstairs. New club?
Maybe one day.
Deb looks around. More space than she's seen in five years.
Did you call Kendall? She quit.
Moved to Hawaii.
(surveying the room) What about Paige?
(stops) It would honestly help if I knew what all of this was for. I know...
I know... I'll tell you
Deb deposits her bag, crosses to Cate. Gives her a kiss on the cheek.
I'll see ya later.
You just got here.
I've been inside for five years. I'm going for a walk.
She turns and heads up the aisle of the theater...
EXT. ART GALLERY - CHELSEA - NIGHT
Debbie walks down a familiar cobblestone street. Though she seems composed, a rage grows within her as she approaches --
INT. KLAUS GUNTHER GALLERY - CHELSEA - CONTINUOUS

Debbie walks into mecca for the downtown art scene. KLAUS GUNTHER makes Gagosian jealous. Sycophants, hipsters and a lot of money. And in the center of it all stands Klaus himself: undeniably handsome, just as oily, he wears a white linen shirt unbuttoned to the jewlery. You should see through this guy but you can't. Debbie grabs a glass of champagne from a waiter and crosses toward him.
Is talking with a group of young women, when he spots Debbie and shifts from charm to restrained terror. He makes quick apologies and starts to leave... Blocks his path.
(frozen) What are you doing here?
Patron of the arts.
I have a restraining order. Good.
She steps really close to him -- an intimate moment -DEBBIE (CONT'D) Do you know what a shiv is? He feels something sharp against his ribs.
Gasps --
I made this one. Proud of it, too. See -- it's important not to melt the toothbrush too much. That way it keeps its length and the strength. So when you stab, you slice up and around.
Debbie drags the shiv up the front of his shirt, towards his neck. To a bystander it might look like a flirtatious move with a large toothpick.
Nice face. Inside you're what we like to call "a pretty girl" -KLAUS
I'll call the police -Deb takes the shiv and slices up the front of his shirt, shredding it. She pops the button on his collar, taking it for a souvenir.
Auf Wiedersehen.
Debbie strides out, still purposeful, while Klaus looks on, terrified.
INT. CHINESE TAKE OUT
All fluorescence and backlit pictures of the dishes. Debbie stands at the counter -- draws a long breath of General Tso's. They hand her a white plastic bag.
INT. THEATER - LATE
It's dark. A few safety lights are on. Deb enters the back of the theater and crosses to a staircase on the side of the stage carrying her take out.
UPSTAIRS LOFT SPACE

It used to be a rehearsal studio. Vast and open with various bits of living area defined by loose furniture. It's chic in a very hard-to-define-kind-of-way. Crosses to the kitchen area, fishes through drawers for a plate and decamps at the small kitchen table. CLOSER She takes out the still-warm white containers and dumps them on her plate. Can't get to the chopsticks fast enough...
Is that all for you?
(turning/mouthful) Oh, hi. Want some. No.
Cate's wearing a silk chinese robe. table. Sits.
She crosses to the
Took a walk. Where?
Deb doesn't answer. They look at each other for a beat and exchange five or six lines of unspoken conversation.
Why would you do something like that? I had to.
No, you didn't. Closure? Bullshit. Oh yeah.
Did he see you? DEBBIE
Debbie takes the shiv out of her purse and shows it to Cate. Toothbrush on one end, lethal blade on the other.
Jesus. (beat) Did you "use it?"
Just a button.
She puts Klaus' button on the table. share the moment. Then suddenly... Cate laughs. They
(calls out) Hey, Luis... You gotta go.
Cate doesn't turn as the young man from the club enters with pants but no shirt. Even more muscles. Even more ink.
I can wait. That's ok. Seriously.
I'll see you soon.
(turns) Seriously. Go.
Luis gets the hints and grabs his shirt, heading downstairs.
Does he have to go?
Cate laughs. Deb slides over the carton of orange chicken.
EXT. EAST VILLAGE - THE NEXT MORNING

The place is waking up from the night before. MEN in ORANGE SUITS hang off the back of trucks collecting trash. CLOSE UP. She's wide awake. Cate's "guest room" is a partitioned space filled with boxes of bootlegged vodka. A certain larcenous charm with a stunning view of the Manhattan Bridge. Deb sits up in bed. Stretches (freedom). She stares out the window at the building across the alley -- seems to pause... FLASHBACK. FOOTAGE)
OCEAN'S ELEVEN (TRACKING SHOT -- ORIGINAL
ZIRGA (CARL REINER) AND TERRY BENEDICT (ANDY GARCIA) walk the casino floor with their respective henchmen behind them. They pass.
...who glances up from his chair at the video poker machine. THE SHOT CONTINUES TO SUDDENLY INCLUDE...
...who stands at the progressive slots holding a bucket of
silver dollars (STILL OCEAN'S ELEVEN -- WE JUST HAVEN'T SEEN THIS BEFORE.) Deb clocks Benedict as he passes, feeds a dollar token into the slot, and pulls the huge lever. Wheels spin...
(jackpot) OH MY GOD...
INT. SUBTERRANEAN CORRIDOR
Benedict strides down the hallway with two employees in tow...
That's not supposed to hit 'til December.
I understand sir.
Did you check the machine?
INT. HOLDING ROOM
Bright neon. No frills. Deb sits on a hard plastic chair. Turns... Looks at Benedict...
(as he enters) Where's my money!
Benedict pauses at the door. Takes her in.
I drove here from Bakersfield.
SERIES OF SHOTS.
OCEAN'S ELEVEN
The swat team carries out the money. The van arrives at the airport. The team meets at the Bellagio fountain.
Bullshit, Danny! SHOT.
She yells into a pay phone.

"Pulled a lever"? You think that's what I did? I "pulled a lever?" (beat) Yeah, and if I hadn't you would have had Terry Benedict up your ass. I bought you time Danny. I deserve the same share as everybody else... (beat) No! Don't you dare hang... (beat/quieter) ...up. Asshole.
DOWN ANGLE.
CENTRAL PARK - DAY (PRESENT)
It's a massive landscape. People stroll. Boats sail. fly. It would take a moment to pick out the two women heading toward the camera. Kites
Honestly when I tell you, it's going to sound a little... Crazy?
Maybe. But there aren't any holes. I've gone through it a million times.
ANGLE.
WOODED PATH
They stroll toward the camera in the dappled sunlight. As they leave the narrow path, Central Park opens in front of them. Fifth Avenue looms. Deb stops -- turns toward Cate. What?
Deb motions in front of her. Trees?
Deb points to...
Rising up above the treeline. CLOSE UP.
She cocks her head slightly, takes in the museum. Then she reaches for Deb's hand and walks her slowly over to a park bench. They sit together.
(deep breath/softly) Look -- I have a friend with a house on Fire Island. He's in Europe for a few months and the place is just sitting empty. You could go out there and stare at the
water and just...
This works, Cate. I'm sure.
It really works.
(pause) That's a museum, Debbie. And they care about the things inside it. That's what makes it a museum.
We're not robbing the museum. Pause...
We're robbing someone in the museum. The museum is just the decoy.
She looks back at it...
INT. MET - AMERICAN WING
They mingle with tourists ambling by the Whistlers and Winslow Homers. John Singer Sargent's 'Madam X' looks down as they walk by...
INT. MET - MAIN FOYER
They stand in front of a sign next to the ticket booths. A sign reads: "CLOSED THE FIRST MONDAY IN MAY."
First Monday in May? Shh.
MET BALL - FILE FOOTAGE
Years of haute couture on display. The richest models in the world wear the most expensive and glamorous fashion. They glide in slow motion past a million strobes as Debbie explains:
It's the biggest event of the year. Hundreds of the richest people gathered under one roof...

They eat a falafel. SERIES OF SHOTS. Deb gestures. Cate nods. (LONG LENS - FAR AWAY) Through the bustle and traffic of New York, Deb explains 'the job'. In subways... Taxi's... Restaurants... With diagrams, gestures, and models made of salt shakers and sugar cubes. Slowly, through it all, Cate's body language subtly changes. She begins, literally, to lean in until... They sit at a table caffeinating in the large open atrium. Wow.
Wow as in yes?
As in: I get it. brilliant.
It's kind of
I had some time on my hands.
It's dangerous...
No doubt. But that's not why I like it. This works.
(weighs it) You know I don't owe you anything.
Just help me figure it out. Then see how you feel. If you don't like it, you don't have to do it. You have an off ramp. There is an irritating, irresistible quality that makes her
Debbie Ocean.
So what's first?
We need a designer.
INT. CATE'S LOFT
They sit in front of the big screen with the documentary playing. They review a large portfolio book of various designers, their work enshrined in plastic.
So I thought about Galliano, but I don't think Anna's gonna let him dress anybody. Right.
There's Largerfeld but it's kind of Dr. Strangelove. Not sure it's the right fit. But here, look at this...
She spins the book. Hovers over it...
Rose Weil? (beat) I know that name.
Huge in the 90's. New Romantic stuff. Edwardian collars. But... (flips a page) Hasn't gone so well lately. She owes the IRS 5 million dollars. Woah.
It's bad. A lien on her assets. Impounded her passport. The bank seized her townhouse.
She sounds perfect.
INT. BACKSTAGE - FASHION SHOW
The name "ROSE WEIL" emblazoned on a wall. After a minute ROSE WEIL, 40's, flustered, lurches toward a trash can, vomiting pre-show.
The show was meant to save her.
INT. FASHION SHOW - BRYANT PARK - EVENING
Amazonian women appear in outfits that suggest a labored attempt at a Victorian theme -- high buttons, lace bodices.
If the new line doesn't take off, she could even be prosecuted.
There is a distracting murmur -- this is not going well. Amongst the unimpressed celebrities, a worried group of middle-aged, Mid-Western Men sit in the front row.
Got a credit line from an Omaha Bank. The only ones who would still loan her money...
They literally are soaked in sweat -- stare perplexed at the
runway at...
Tough way to get into fashion.
THE LAST MODEL

Strides out in a corset with over abundance metal buckles BDSM meets Pirates of the Caribbean. She turns to the top of the runway to introduce Rose, but no one appears. Finally, one of the models drags her on-stage to take a bow before a half-empty tent.
INT. BACKSTAGE - FASHION SHOW - LATER

The silent break-down of a failed show: Naked models down champagne, designer detritus covers the floor -- no one scurries to pick it up. Cate wanders through the room, searching for Rose. She pauses at the sound of SOBS coming from the other side of a closet door. Cate opens it to find a weeping Rose, sitting on the floor, eating Nutella.
(beat) It was gorgeous.
(looks up/tear stained) It was shite.
I think that's a little harsh.
"Derivative of myself." That's what they say. Do you know what a hell that is? Resenting who you were because you'll never be that again -(shovels Nutella) How did I get here?
(sinks to the floor/gently) You spent 18 million dollars in two years. You had two house boats on the Seine.
Cate takes her hand.
(softly) I know. And they were divine. (a beat) I'm old.
You're not old -ROSE WEIL I'm old and -(heaves) I'm going to go to prison -- and (heaves) And then I'm going to be poor...
Not necessarily.
Rose looks up.
What if I could make all of this go away. Even get your passport back.
Long pause -ROSE WEIL
Dress Daphne Kluger for the Met Ball.
Rose stares up her.
Are you real? Very.
(means it) You're an angel.
Cate shrugs. She'll take it.
INT. PRESS CONFERENCE - DAY

PAPARAZZI FLASH -- As the glare recedes, a stunning face is revealed like a developing photograph -- DAPHNE KLUGER, 30, goofy and aloof and devastatingly charming. She's been famous for a while and she likes it. Daphne soaks up the flash as the Chairwoman of the Costume Institute introduces
The Met Ball is all about grace and

The crowd erupts in laughter, heads thrown back and shoulders rising like a ballet of bobble-heads. Except for Cate and Debbie, seated in the back row, straight-faced. A REPORTER raises his hand -REPORTER Do you know who you're wearing?
Right now? (she looks under her shirt) Under Armour?
No, who is dressing you?
I normally do it myself unless I'm really wasted.
Don't you guys have something better to worry about -- like climate change? (they're not budging) (MORE)
I promise the moment we decide on a designer you'll be the first to know.
ANGLE.
BACK OF THE ROOM
Debbie and Cate exchange a look. INT. CATE'S LOFT - DEBBIE, CATE, AND ROSE WEIL They leaf through pictures of Daphne Kluger.
Oh. She's sort of divine. bust.
Maybe something simple. velvet with no other...
Just
Maybe this.
She tosses an embossed photo on the table. CLOSE UP PHOTO. Rose leans in. THE HARRINGTON NECKLACE
Oh my.
Even the photo glistens.
It's called the Harrington necklace. Originally commissioned for an American oil magnate. Weighs over six pounds. Texans.
When he died they sold it back to Tiffany's who've kept it in their vault ever since.
They never let it out. Oh.
Then how do we...
They might let it out for her.
This years theme is Starry Night. A million jewels glittering in the night sky.
And if you were to insist... behalf of Daphne Kluger... Ooooh...
Through all of her haze, Rose begins to get it...
It's a lovely piece. No shit.
It kicks prisms of light all over the frame -- perfect little rainbows. OVER THIS -- IN HINDI:
(This isn't flawless.) WIDER.
BHALLA JEWELERY INC. STATEN ISLAND

AMITA (Mindy Kaling) holds a large diamond up to her loupe. Her mother, elegant, glamorous, dripping in jewels, hovers close by as she examines it. Behind them are half a dozen other jewlers at a long work bench -- all Indian as well -all family. There are glass jars of candy -- everywhere.
(It's a good stone. What's the
(I work here -- stop selling me.)
EXT. STATEN ISLAND FERRY
Debbie stands at the railing staring out over the water as they head past Ellis Island.
She's like family. My dad used to fence stuff through the store.
The island approaches...
INT. BHALLA JEWLERS - DAY
(This diamond is a K -- at best.)
(It's an H -- )
(You're out of your mind.)
(I'm concerned about your vision)
(This is piss yellow --)
(Why are you so vulgar. If your father was alive...)
(Grade it yourself!)
(This is why you have no husband.)
Amita looks up, with the loupe on.
AMITA'S POV -- THROUGH THE LOUPE
Debbie (highly magnified) stands out the window -- waving, signaling for Amita to join her.
EXT. STATEN ISLAND PROMENADE - MOMENTS LATER
Amita approaches Debbie -AMITA What're you doing here?
Sorry to hear about your pops.
Yeah, thanks... (re: her mother/sotto) Sometimes I wish she'd join him. (beat) Sorry about Danny.
Yeah well... I got something for you. A job.
I heard that didn't work out too well. Happens.
What? You want to run some stuff through the store?
Little more than that. (beat) How long would it take you to make 8 pieces of jewelry? If the stones are already cut.
I don't know. Six hours?
What if I told you didn't have to live with your mother anymore?
Amita raises an eyebrow...
EXT. STATEN ISLAND FERRY
Deb stands at the rail on her cell phone.
(into the phone) Yeah. She's all good. But we may have to put her up for a while. Had a fight with her mom.
INT. CATE'S LOFT

A 23 year old GIRL (huge dreads and lots of tats) sits cross legged in a knoll womb chair with an industrial looking computer on her lap. Cate sits across from her staring as she pounds away at her keyboard.
(suddenly) Wow. What?
The girl doesn't answer but stays focused on her screen. Cate leans forward, paying rapt attention.
I am never taking the F train again.
Debbie comes bounding through the doorway with loads of energy.
(sees the girl) Hi there.
What?
She's "in." In where?
Is this what you mean? just a lot of vases. There's
Cate leans over and looks at her screen. Yeah.
That's the Egyptian wing.
